channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Leigh stay on course for Play-offs after Castleford win
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Highlights of the Super League match between Leigh Leopards and Castleford Tigers.

item.title: Osun 2026: APC’s technocracy takes on Accord’s populism
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Osun State is set to close the off-cycle election calendar on August 15, 2026. This upcoming election will give voters a critical opportunity to choose a new leader and chart the socioeconomic course of the state for the next four years. For decades, power in the state has alternated between two bit

item.title: BBC to show England's World Cup semi-final
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: England's World Cup semi-final against Argentina or Switzerland will be broadcast live on BBC One and BBC iPlayer.

item.title: Nigerians detained in Ethiopia in limbo after repatriation treaty – Okoye, Coordinator, TBNI
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Lawyer and National Coordinator, Take Back Nigeria Initiative (TBNI), Abumchukwu N. Okoye, says he is deeply disturbed that the initial wave of joy, which lifted the spirits of Nigerians wrongly detained in Ethiopia is fast turning into despair. The post Nigerians detained in Ethiopia in limbo after

item.title: Indian tourists killed after speedboat capsizes in Vietnam
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Fifteen Indian tourists have died after their speedboat capsized off Vietnam’s Phu Quoc Island.

item.title: Whistleblower claims Kennedy Center ‘rushed’ renovations to please Trump
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Democratic Senator Sheldon Whitehouse details rusting columns, uneven paint and unnecessary demolition under Trump.

item.title: Cathie Wood buys $22.8 million of surging tech stock
item.dc:creator: Yahoo Finance

item.title: Iran says Strait of Hormuz closed 'until further notice'
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The IRGC says it fired a warning shot at a vessel in the strait and is warning the US not to respond with "aggression".

item.title: FIFA to sell pieces of World Cup final stadium pitch, could earn millions
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Pieces from the turf used at the stadium will go on sale as memorabilia and will be priced from $450 to $1,200.

item.title: President Federation Cup: El-Kanemi Beat Ikorodu City To Clinch 4th Title
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: In a tense, rain-soaked final at the Stephen Keshi Stadium in Asaba on Saturday, El-Kanemi Warriors etched their name deeper into Nigerian football history, clinching their fourth President Federation Cup crown with a gritty 1-0 triumph over gallant Ikorodu City. The solitary strike, a coolly conver

item.title: In Trinidad and Tobago, Where Africa and India Meet
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The community pooled money to purchase the freedom of other Muslims and reportedly numbered about 140 by the 1830s. Bath unsuccessfully petitioned the British authorities to repatriate the community to Africa.

item.title: UK Confirms Visa Requirement For Over 100 Countries Visiting Under 6 Months
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The United Kingdom has updated its Immigration Rules Appendix Visitor, reaffirming the list of countries and categories of travellers required to obtain a visa before travelling to the UK for visits or any stay of less than six months. The updated guidance, released by the UK Home Office on July 1 a

item.title: Weightlifting beats running for blood sugar control, researchers find

item.title: NIDO Launches Nationwide Campaign Against Human Trafficking In Schools
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Nigerians in Diaspora Organisation (NIDO) has launched a nationwide awareness campaign aimed at protecting secondary school students from human trafficking and educating them on how to identify and avoid traffickers’ tactics. The campaign began in Makurdi, Benue State, with a sensitisation progr

item.title: Fifa official refuses to answer BBC's Balogun ban questions
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: The head of Fifa's disciplinary committee refuses to answer BBC questions about the decision-making process which led to United States striker Folarin Balogun avoiding a ban after being sent off.

item.title: Zamfara Partners Security Agencies To Enforce Child Protection Law
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Zamfara State Government has reaffirmed its commitment to working closely with security agencies and other stakeholders to strengthen child protection and ensure the strict enforcement of the Zamfara State Child Protection Law, 2022, against anyone found violating the rights of children.

item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: CR-SEMA confirmed the casualties on Saturday during a rapid damage assessment of the affected community conducted in collaboration with officials of the Disaster Management Unit of the Nigerian Red Cross Society.

item.title: Argentina midfield great Rattin dies aged 89
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Argentina great Antonio Rattin, whose sending-off at the 1966 World Cup led to the introduction of red and yellow cards, dies aged 89.

item.title: Federation Cup Final: El-Kanemi outclass Ikorodu City to claim fourth title
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: It was a disappointing end to the season for Ikorodu City. The Lagos club not only lost the cup final but had also missed the chance to qualify for continental football on the final day of the Nigeria Premier Football League season.

item.title: ₦228bn: The Day The North Decided To Buy Back Its Peace
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: …For years the North spoke in resolutions. On Monday in Kaduna, it spoke in naira. Under the watch of host Governor Senator Uba Sani, the 19 Northern governors sat with the Northern Traditional Rulers’ Council and did what no region has done before: they voted to fund their own security,

item.title: Italy icon Maldini national team’s technical director
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Italian football icon Paolo Maldini has been appointed technical director of the national football federation (FIGC) looking to rebuild after Italy's failure to qualify for a third consecutive World Cup. The post Italy icon Maldini national team’s technical director appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Bulldozers move in as Sanwo-Olu declares war on illegal highway markets, shanties
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The governor said the clearance operation aligns with the state's plan to transform the Lagos-Badagry Expressway into a modern 10-lane highway capable of supporting increased traffic and improving transportation across the metropolis.

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC), Peter Obi, has dismissed Works Minister David Umahi’s call for a public debate, saying the minister must first become a presidential candidate before seeking such an engagement.   Umahi had challenged Obi to a

item.title: This slushie machine was a lifesaver during NYC’s heat wave
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: Last weekend’s brutal NYC heat wave had me craving a frozen drink almost every afternoon. Normally, that would mean sweating through a walk to 7-Eleven for a slurpee. This time, though, I stayed home and put the new Ninja Slushi Twist to the test. Ninja’s latest slushie machine builds on

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The abducted pastor of the Nongo u Kristu u i Ser u sha Tar (NKST) Church, Rev. Samuel Gbinde, has died shortly after regaining freedom from captivity in Benue State. Rev. Gbinde and two members of his congregation were abducted on June 30, 2026, when armed men invaded his residence at the NKST Chur

item.title: Polish PM pledges memorial to victims of WW2 'genocide by Ukrainian nationalists'
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Warsaw and Kyiv have for decades been at loggerheads over the killings of many thousands of civilians during World War Two.

item.title: US judge dismisses January 6 case against Proud Boys after Trump order
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The case is the latest instance of the Trump administration seeking to unravel prosecutions against January 6 rioters.

item.description: Nasarawa Amazons FC have emerged champions of the 2026 Women’s President Federation Cup after defeating Heartland Queens FC of Owerri The victory came through penalty shootout at the Stephen Keshi Stadium in Asaba. item.description: Rate the Norway and England players out of 10 below and come back 30 minutes after full-time to see the final ratings.

item.title: Global population at 8.3 billion: What does that mean for anyone under 30?

On World Population Day 2026, the spotlight is on empowering youth to shape their futures through education, health, and opportunity. From family planning

item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: "There was once I read an article about a young graduate who said he didn't get a job. He said he sells akara because he couldn't get a job. We approached him, but I didn't put my name to it. We equipped him more. He now has 12 workers working under him, and he's doing very, very well," she said.

item.title: DSS frees Sowore’s media aide detained over drone
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Department of State Services, DSS, has released Omoyele Sowore’s media aide, Zainab Sodiq, to the leadership of the International Press Institute (IPI Nigeria). Sodiq was arrested and detained by the Department of State Services (DSS) over her possession of a drone. In a statement issued o

item.title: Modi Courts Indo-Pacific Partners as China, US Reshape Region
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: From missiles and uranium to critical minerals and sports diplomacy, Prime Minister Narendra Modi used a three-nation tour to deepen India’s Indo-Pacific partnerships as regional powers strengthen ties amid China’s growing influence and uncertainty over US engagement.

The final match, played at the Asaba Stadium in Delta State, saw the Lafia Landladies clinch t

He said the former Anambra State governor would be alive to contest the election and suffer another defeat at the polls. Umahi spoke while inspecting ongoing fed

The blogger had published four news articles

Reacting to the news, FIFA President Gianni Infantino, in a statement, said Adams would be sorely missed. “It&#821

item.description: English Premier League club, Chelsea,on Saturday, announced the death of Ken Bates, the club’s former chairman. item.description: "We appreciate the efforts of all the security agencies in securing the release of our school children and their teachers from captivity. Congratulations to each of them and their families. Our God answers prayers. Hallelujah!!!" the statement added.

item.title: Ogun corporation attributes Abeokuta water disruption to road projects
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The Ogun State Water Corporation (OGSWC) dismissed claims that the French Development Agency (AFD)-funded Ogun Urban Water Supply Project failed to achieve its objectives The post Ogun corporation attributes Abeokuta water disruption to road projects appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Police Arrest Five Suspected Cultists in Lagos
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Lagos State Police Command has arrested five suspected cultists accused of terrorising residents in Apapa, Ijora and Badia areas of the state. The Command’s Violent Crime Response Unit (VCRU) carried out the operation following credible intelligence, according to a statement issued on Satu

item.title: Oyebamiji Applauds Tinubu, Security Forces Over Rescue Of Orire Pupils, Teachers
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The All Progressives Congress (APC) governorship candidate for the August 8, 2026 Osun State gubernatorial election, Asiwaju Munirudeen Bola Oyebamiji (AMBO), has congratulated the pupils, teachers and other victims recently rescued after spending more than 50 days in captivity, following their abdu

item.title: South African World Cup star found dead
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: South Africa’s sports ministry has announced the death of Bafana Bafana midfielder Jayden Adams. he Mamelodi Sundowns player’s was found dead in his home, and cause of death is still unclear. Adams was part of South Africa’s team to the ongoing 2026 FIFA World Cup where he featured in all three grou

item.title: World Cup 2026: Spain Capable Of Beating France –De La Fuente
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Spain manager Luis de la Fuente has disclosed that he is completely convinced his squad will beat France in their FIFA World Cup 2026 semi-final clash. Recall that Spain advanced on a winning goal by substitute Mikel Merino in the 88th minute of the quarterfinals at SoFi Stadium. La Roja conceded it

item.title: “I don’t want to talk to Adeboye; a pastor must rebuke a bad government” — Odumeje
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The popular Onitsha-based cleric Chukwuemeka “Odumeje” Ohanaemere has offered his view on the role of religious leaders in holding government accountable The post “I don’t want to talk to Adeboye; a pastor must rebuke a bad government” — Odumeje appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Norway-England live: Three Lions take the lead through Bellingham's extra-time goal
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Norway and England clash in a quarter-final encounter at the World Cup, with both nations seeking to reach the semi-finals. The Three Lions aim to extend their knockout campaign, while the Scandinavians look to upset the odds against one of world football's traditional powers. Follow our liveblog fo

item.title: England, Norway battle heat as Argentina face Swiss in World Cup last eight
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: England and Norway were preparing to lock horns in a blockbuster World Cup quarter-final battle of elite strikers in Miami on Saturday set to be played in ferocious heat and humidity. The post England, Norway battle heat as Argentina face Swiss in World Cup last eight appeared first on Vanguard News

item.title: What’s behind Pakistan’s mass deportation of Afghans?
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Islamabad cites national security concerns, returns thousands of undocumented migrants.
